novel_features.name: Opportunity rank
novel_features.description: Rank niches across all four buckets at once by a composite of estimated revenue, sales, and price.
novel_features.rationale: The web UI only shows one bucket at a time; ranking across the full synced set needs the local store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when an agent needs the single best niche by opportunity, not a bucket-by-bucket browse.

novel_features.name: Revenue drift
novel_features.description: Show which synced niches are rising or fading in estimated revenue versus an earlier snapshot.
novel_features.rationale: The API is point-in-time only and has no history endpoint; trend exists only because we snapshot on every sync.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this for timing decisions (publish now vs skip) that the source tool cannot answer at all.

novel_features.name: Cross-bucket dedup
novel_features.description: Find books that appear in more than one niche bucket (same ASIN) and show which buckets.
novel_features.rationale: Requires an ASIN-derived join across buckets in local SQLite that no single API call exposes.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this to spot niches surfacing in multiple buckets, a strong cross-validated signal.

novel_features.name: Publisher saturation
novel_features.description: Per bucket, show how concentrated estimated revenue is among publishers (whale vs fragmented).
novel_features.rationale: The API exposes a publisher field but no concentration metric; the signal is computed locally.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this to tell an open niche from one a single publisher already dominates.

novel_features.name: Competitor inspect
novel_features.description: For a focus book, list same-publisher and same-price-band competitors using the extracted ASIN.
novel_features.rationale: Reverse-ASIN-style competitor grouping is a local join the web UI does not offer.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this to study who else is winning a specific niche before committing to it.

novel_features.name: KDP keyword CSV export
novel_features.description: Export title, ASIN, price, estimated sales, and revenue as CSV for KDP backend keyword and cover work.
novel_features.rationale: CSV export is a table-stakes gap the web tool lacks; emitted from the local store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this to hand a shortlist off to keyword/cover tooling without retyping titles.

novel_features.name: Title keyword frequency
novel_features.description: Tokenize synced book titles and count keyword frequency to surface hot terms for a niche.
novel_features.rationale: Mechanical title-token aggregation over the local store, no LLM and no upstream support.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this to seed KDP backend keyword fields from what is actually selling.

narrative.headline: Every KDP Niche Finder bucket as a local research database — rank niches across buckets, track whether revenue is rising or fading, and export shortlists, none of which the web tool does.
narrative.value_prop: KDP Niche Finder is a click-to-browse web tool that shows one curated niche bucket at a time with no history and no export. This CLI mirrors all four buckets into local SQLite so you can rank niches by opportunity across buckets (rank), see which niches are rising or fading since your last refresh (drift), spot books that appear in multiple buckets (dupes), gauge publisher saturation, and export KDP-ready CSVs — all offline and agent-native.
narrative.auth_narrative: KDP Niche Finder uses a Laravel session login (no API key). Run `kdpnichefinder-pp-cli auth login --chrome` to capture your logged-in kdpnichefinder.com browser session; reads use the session cookie and saves/folders additionally send the CSRF token the CLI composes for you.

quickstart.comment: Check config and reachability before anything else (no auth needed).
Mirror all four niche buckets into the local store (also snapshots revenue for drift).
Rank the best low-price niches across every bucket at once.
After a second refresh on a later day, see which niches are gaining revenue.

troubleshoots.fix: Your session expired; re-run `kdpnichefinder-pp-cli auth login --chrome`.
drift needs at least two refreshes on different dates; run `refresh` again tomorrow.

troubleshoots.symptom: Saving a book fails with a CSRF/419 error
troubleshoots.fix: Re-run `auth login --chrome` to refresh the XSRF token cookie.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when an agent or power user needs to research Amazon KDP niches programmatically: ranking opportunities across all buckets, tracking niche revenue trends over time, studying publisher competition, or exporting shortlists. It turns KDP Niche Finder's browse-only web UI into a queryable local database.
anti_triggers: Do not use this CLI to generate book covers or interiors — that is Artistly's generation suite, not this niche tool.
Do not use it for live Amazon BSR lookups of arbitrary ASINs — it only covers the curated KDP Niche Finder buckets.
Do not use it for non-US marketplaces — the source data is US-centric.

recipes.title: Best low-price niche across all buckets
recipes.explanation: Ranks every synced book by revenue-per-dollar so cheap, high-return niches surface first.

recipes.title: Narrow a verbose niche list to the fields that matter
recipes.explanation: Uses dotted --select paths to pull just title, revenue, and Amazon link from the paginated bucket response.

recipes.title: Spot rising niches week over week
recipes.explanation: Diffs the latest snapshot against one a week ago to show niches gaining estimated revenue.

recipes.title: Export a saved shortlist for keyword work
recipes.explanation: Emits title/ASIN/price/sales/revenue as CSV for KDP backend keyword and cover tooling.
